paper: Corporate and Other Laws  |  Master Test 02

crosses.for: doesn't need to carry forward losses

remarks.title: Part I
lines: 13 of 15. Q4 option is incorrect: key is B, you wrote C.
All other seven MCQs are correct and earned full marks.

remarks.title: Registration of charge
lines: Earned: 1 mark for Section 77 duty to register within 30 days.
Earned: 2 marks for stating the consequences (loss of priority, void against liquidator/creditor).
Not earned: 2 marks. The trigger point is when the charge is actually created (documentation done),
not just when the limit is sanctioned by the bank.

remarks.title: Foreign register
lines: Earned: 1 mark for stating that the company can keep a foreign register outside India.
Not earned: 4 marks. You wrote 15 days instead of the correct 30 days as per Rule 7.
Because of the wrong time limit, your final conclusion was also incorrect.

remarks.title: Interim dividend
lines: Earned: 2 marks for stating that interim dividend can be declared out of current year's profit
and mentioning the average of 3 years in case of loss.
Not earned: 3 marks. Under the third proviso, previous losses and depreciation MUST be set off.
Your conclusion that the company doesn't need to carry forward losses/depreciation is wrong.

remarks.title: Consolidated financial statements
lines: Earned: 4 marks for Section 129 requirement, listing the 3 exemption conditions,
and concluding correctly for Madan Pvt Ltd (part i).
Not earned: 1 mark for part (ii). If the company were listed, it would NOT qualify for the exemption,
because one of the conditions is that the company must be unlisted.

remarks.title: Charge on assets outside India
lines: Earned: 5 / 5 marks. Complete and accurate. You correctly noted the duty to register
on all assets (tangible or intangible, inside or outside India) and rightly concluded
that the company's claim is incorrect.

remarks.title: EGM Requisition
lines: Earned: 5 / 5 marks. Excellent presentation. Section 100, 10% voting power requirement,
and the 12.5% calculation were all perfectly stated, leading to the correct conclusion.

remarks.title: Unpaid Dividend Account and IEPF
lines: Earned: 4 marks for explaining the 7-year timeline to transfer to UDA and then IEPF,
and for advising Manish to claim via IEPF-5.
Warning: You forgot to quote Section 124. Citing the section is important for full clarity.

rating.name: Introduction Competence
rating.note: You often miss writing the Section numbers (like Section 124). Always cite sections.

rating.name: Content Coverage
rating.note: Most legal points covered well, but you missed the depreciation condition in Q1(c).

rating.name: Conclusion Competence
rating.note: Good generally, but Q1(a), Q1(b), and Q1(c) conclusions had factual errors based on misremembered limits/rules.

rating.name: Presentation & Structure
rating.note: Excellent handwriting and clear point-wise structure.

rating.name: Language Proficiency
rating.note: A few grammatical slips, but legal terminology is standard.

feedback.head: WHAT YOU DID WELL
lines: Your presentation is very neat and readable. Point-wise layout is perfect.
You handled the EGM Requisition (Q3(a)) and Registration of charge (Q2(b)) brilliantly,
securing full marks.

feedback.head: FIX THESE HABITS
lines: *1. Quote Section numbers.*
You lost out on a mark in Q3(b) for missing Section 124. Memorizing key sections helps.
*2. Be precise with timelines and provisos.*
In Q1(b), writing 15 days instead of 30 cost you heavily. In Q1(c), missing the depreciation set-off rule derailed your conclusion.

feedback.head: STRATEGY FOR BETTER MARKS
lines: Always verify all conditions before jumping to a conclusion.
For example, in Q2(a) part (ii), you missed that listing immediately disqualifies a company from the CFS exemption.

footer: Connect with an SME if you would like to discuss this feedback in detail.
